message:
✨ Enable programmatic Copilot assignment via REST API

Use copilot-swe-agent[bot] as the assignee to trigger Copilot
coding agent automatically when triage/accepted label is added.

Changes:
- Use REST API POST to /issues/{number}/assignees
- Check for "Copilot" in response to verify assignment
- Update labels to ai-awaiting-fix when Copilot is working

Source: https://github.blog/changelog/2025-12-03-assign-issues-to-copilot-using-the-api/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -94,80 +94,40 @@ jobs:
 
           echo "Issue ID: $ISSUE_ID"
 
-          # Get Copilot agent ID from assignable users (includes bots)
-          COPILOT_ID=$(gh api graphql -f query="
-            query {
-              repository(owner: \"$REPO_OWNER\", name: \"$REPO_NAME\") {
-                assignableUsers(first: 100, query: \"copilot\") {
-                  nodes {
-                    login
-                    id
-                  }
-                }
-              }
-            }" --jq '.data.repository.assignableUsers.nodes[] | select(.login == "copilot-swe-agent") | .id')
-
-          echo "Copilot ID: $COPILOT_ID"
+          # Assign Copilot to the issue using the REST API
+          # The assignee must be "copilot-swe-agent[bot]" (with [bot] suffix)
+          echo "Assigning Copilot to issue #$ISSUE_NUM..."
 
-          # If not found via assignableUsers, try the global bot lookup
-          if [ -z "$COPILOT_ID" ]; then
-            echo "Trying global user lookup for copilot-swe-agent..."
-            COPILOT_ID=$(gh api graphql -f query="
-              query {
-                user(login: \"copilot-swe-agent\") {
-                  id
-                }
-              }" --jq '.data.user.id' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
-            echo "Global lookup result: $COPILOT_ID"
-          fi
-
-          if [ -z "$COPILOT_ID" ]; then
-            echo "::warning::Copilot agent not found. Copilot may not be enabled for this repository."
-            echo "copilot_available=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
-            exit 0
+          ASSIGN_RESULT=$(gh api repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues/$ISSUE_NUM/assignees \
+            -X POST \
+            --input - <<< '{"assignees": ["copilot-swe-agent[bot]"]}' 2>&1) || true
+
+          # Check if Copilot was successfully assigned
+          if echo "$ASSIGN_RESULT" | grep -q '"login":"Copilot"'; then
+            echo "Copilot successfully assigned to issue #$ISSUE_NUM"
+            echo "copilot_assigned=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+          else
+            echo "::warning::Failed to assign Copilot. Response: $ASSIGN_RESULT"
+            echo "copilot_assigned=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
           fi
 
-          echo "Copilot is available for this repository"
-          echo "copilot_available=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
-
-          # Note: Copilot cannot be programmatically assigned via public GitHub APIs.
-          # The "Assign to Copilot" button in the UI uses an internal API.
-          # We'll add a comment instructing maintainers to click the button.
-
-          COMMENT_BODY='🤖 **Ready for Copilot**
-
-          This issue has been approved for AI-assisted resolution. GitHub Copilot SWE Agent is available for this repository.
-
-          **To start the AI fix:**
-          1. Click the **"Assign to Copilot"** button in the Development section (right sidebar)
-          2. Or click **"Code with agent mode"** to start a Copilot coding session
-
-          Copilot will analyze this issue and create a pull request with the proposed fix.
-
-          ---
-          *This message was automatically generated by the AI Fix workflow.*'
-
-          gh issue comment "$ISSUE_NUM" --repo "${{ github.repository }}" --body "$COMMENT_BODY"
-
-          echo "Posted instructions for Copilot assignment"
-
-      - name: Update labels - copilot available
-        if: steps.assign_copilot.outputs.copilot_available == 'true'
+      - name: Update labels - copilot assigned
+        if: steps.assign_copilot.outputs.copilot_assigned == 'true'
         env:
           GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
         run: |
           REPO="${{ github.repository }}"
           ISSUE="${{ steps.issue.outputs.number }}"
 
           # Create label if it doesn't exist
-          gh label create "ai-ready-for-copilot" --repo "$REPO" --color "6f42c1" --description "Ready for Copilot assignment" 2>/dev/null || true
+          gh label create "ai-awaiting-fix" --repo "$REPO" --color "6f42c1" --description "Copilot is working on a fix" 2>/dev/null || true
 
-          # Update status: remove processing, add ready for copilot
+          # Update status: remove processing, add awaiting fix
           gh issue edit "$ISSUE" --repo "$REPO" --remove-label "ai-processing" 2>/dev/null || true
-          gh issue edit "$ISSUE" --repo "$REPO" --add-label "ai-ready-for-copilot"
+          gh issue edit "$ISSUE" --repo "$REPO" --add-label "ai-awaiting-fix"
 
-      - name: Handle Copilot unavailable
-        if: steps.assign_copilot.outputs.copilot_available != 'true'
+      - name: Handle Copilot assignment failed
+        if: steps.assign_copilot.outputs.copilot_assigned != 'true'
         env:
           GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
         run: |
